scientific_question: Does the exact solver-native derivation DAG that produces each shallow R20 learned clause contain cross-corpus candidate information that is absent from clause identity, clause-output topology and raw trajectory magnitudes?

new_observation_boundary.source: CaDiCaL 3.0 public LRAT proof-tracer interface with antecedents enabled
new_observation_boundary.unit_of_observation: every solver-derived clause and its exact original-or-derived parent clause IDs
new_observation_boundary.freshness: one freshly copied solver per candidate cell with the tracer connected before the irredundant formula copy
new_observation_boundary.exact_parent_cover: every positive antecedent ID is classified as original or previously derived; any missing parent rejects the measurement
new_observation_boundary.streaming_reduction: exact event-by-event DAG traversal is reduced online; raw multi-gigabyte event text is never the scientific object
fixed_per_horizon_feature_families: candidate-relative direct assumption agreement and opposition
transitive assumption-ancestry masks and edge counts
parent reuse concentration entropy and maximum fanout
solver proof lifecycle events including deletion demotion weakening strengthening and finalization
derived_ratio_features: opposition fraction among direct assumption literals
all_horizon_primary_signals: event counts for all redundant and irredundant partitions
redundant direct-touch ancestry-touch agreement opposition parent-ancestry and recurrence counts
redundant clause-size antecedent-fanin and proof-depth means and maxima
all and redundant parent-reuse normalized entropy reused-reference fraction and maximum use
all_horizon_composition: sum over the four incremental horizons for each primary signal
h8 minus h1 signed change for each primary signal
maximum over the four horizons for each primary signal
new_observation_boundary.ties: Borda baseline rank then cell index

engineering_probe_disclosure.purpose: verify API completeness throughput and streaming representation size only

calibration_contract.block_mapping: A359 blocks 0-1, A363 blocks 2-3, A367 blocks 4-7; source index floor-divided by 16
calibration_contract.outer_crossfit: hold out one complete four-target block; feature selection and aggregation use the other 28 targets only

direct_frontier_gate: selected operator is proof-derived rather than borda_sum_baseline
selected operator is positive in all eight held blocks
selected minimum held-block gain is at least the selected-subset Borda minimum
mechanism_scale_gate: at least one proof-derived operator is positive in all eight held blocks
its minimum held-block gain is positive
its balanced eight-block gain is at least 0.25 bits
scale_decision.if_either_gate_passes: instrument the complete 128-target calibration corpus and only then open target-blind W52 proof tracing
scale_decision.if_both_gates_fail: retain the exact solver-native antecedent boundary and follow the strongest typed subgraph or conflict-horizon interaction rather than reweighting clause outputs
